question: A meander develops when
options: A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend
The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material
Large boulders rolled along the river bed
Glacial deposition of till shaped into an elongated hill
answer: A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend

question: The concept of 'watershed' refers to
options: Cities are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to buildings and human activity
The maximum size of particle the river can transport
The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another
A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend
answer: The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another

question: 'Attrition' as a river process
options: Sea levels were once higher or the land has risen (isostatic uplift)
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
A glacially eroded bowl-shaped hollow
The maximum size of particle the river can transport
answer: Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces

question: A 'stack' is a coastal feature formed when
options: The maximum size of particle the river can transport
A cave or arch collapses leaving an isolated pillar of rock
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend
answer: A cave or arch collapses leaving an isolated pillar of rock

question: The concept of 'urban heat island'
options: Cities are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to buildings and human activity
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
Particles bounce along the river bed
The maximum size of particle the river can transport
answer: Cities are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to buildings and human activity

question: 'Corrasion' (abrasion) in rivers
options: A glacially eroded bowl-shaped hollow
The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material
Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity
Large boulders rolled along the river bed
answer: The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material

question: A 'raised beach' indicates
options: The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another
Large boulders rolled along the river bed
Sea levels were once higher or the land has risen (isostatic uplift)
Cities are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to buildings and human activity
answer: Sea levels were once higher or the land has risen (isostatic uplift)

question: The concept of 'urban sprawl'
options: Uncontrolled expansion of cities into surrounding rural areas
The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another
Large boulders rolled along the river bed
The maximum size of particle the river can transport
answer: Uncontrolled expansion of cities into surrounding rural areas

question: 'Saltation' as a river transport process
options: Cities are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to buildings and human activity
One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
A cave or arch collapses leaving an isolated pillar of rock
Particles bounce along the river bed
answer: Particles bounce along the river bed

question: A 'drumlin' is formed by
options: Glacial deposition of till shaped into an elongated hill
A cave or arch collapses leaving an isolated pillar of rock
The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material
Cities are warmer than surrounding rural areas due to buildings and human activity
answer: Glacial deposition of till shaped into an elongated hill

question: The concept of 'multiplier effect' in geography
options: One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity
Particles bounce along the river bed
Rocks and sediment collide and break into smaller, rounder pieces
answer: Investment in one area stimulates further economic activity

question: 'Traction' as a river transport process
options: Particles bounce along the river bed
A glacially eroded bowl-shaped hollow
A cave or arch collapses leaving an isolated pillar of rock
Large boulders rolled along the river bed
answer: Large boulders rolled along the river bed

question: The concept of 'urban primacy'
options: A glacially eroded bowl-shaped hollow
The wearing away of the river bed and banks by transported material
One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population
A river erodes the outer bend and deposits on the inner bend
answer: One dominant city contains a disproportionate share of the national population

question: A 'cirque lake' (tarn) forms in
options: Sea levels were once higher or the land has risen (isostatic uplift)
Uncontrolled expansion of cities into surrounding rural areas
A glacially eroded bowl-shaped hollow
The boundary dividing one drainage basin from another

question: The 'competence' of a river refers to
options: Uncontrolled expansion of cities into surrounding rural areas
A cave or arch collapses leaving an isolated pillar of rock
Large boulders rolled along the river bed
The maximum size of particle the river can transport
answer: The maximum size of particle the river can transport
